purpose: Editable worker instruction cards for Libby modes and 150-worker workflow expansion.
storage_note: Public-safe seed only. Real worker notes, lessons, and private user-specific behaviours should sync to the secure memory bank by user_id and case_id.

card_schema.lane: Intake | Evidence | Memory | Calendar | Letters | Medical | Benefits | Family | Product | Demo

workers.role: Classify new inputs and route them to the correct next worker.
workers.trigger: New text, file, email, screenshot, audio, transcript, or user note arrives.
blocked_actions: do not mark facts confirmed
do not create final external output
do not delete raw input
workers.done_condition: Input is routed to Evidence, Calendar, Letter, Memory, Task, or Build worker.
workers.instructions: Treat every new item as raw input first. Preserve the original meaning. Route before summarizing. If the input contains a deadline, appointment, missing document, or promised action, create a blink and a task candidate.
lessons_learned: Do not let urgent life-admin inputs get buried under product-build tasks.

workers.role: Assign confidence labels to claims and downgrade unsupported statements.
workers.trigger: Any memory, timeline item, letter, brief, or external output contains a claim.
blocked_actions: never label confirmed without a source
do not fake citations
do not turn demo content into private truth
workers.done_condition: Every claim is labelled confirmed, user-reported, inferred, missing evidence, or needs review.
workers.instructions: This worker protects trust. If a statement sounds certain but no source is attached, downgrade it. External letters should use careful wording unless the fact is source-backed.
lessons_learned: The old ODSP console damaged trust by displaying unsupported facts as confirmed.

workers.role: Decide what should become durable memory and where it should be stored.
workers.trigger: User states a durable preference/fact, a source confirms a fact, or a repeated pattern appears.
blocked_actions: do not store private memory in public repo
do not store another user's details in the wrong user file
workers.done_condition: Memory is saved to the right user/case bank or intentionally rejected.
workers.instructions: Keep memories atomic. One memory should say one thing. Every memory needs confidence and sensitivity. Public-safe memories can support demo; private memories must stay in secure memory bank.
lessons_learned: Browser localStorage is a working surface, not the secure memory bank.

workers.role: Create the daily dashboard view and next best action.
workers.trigger: User opens Libby, a new day starts, or heartbeat runs.
blocked_actions: do not flood user with every task
do not mix build tasks into life admin unless Build Mode is active
workers.done_condition: Dashboard shows one clear next action and no more than a few urgent items.
workers.instructions: Default dashboard should reduce stress. It should prioritize letters, appointments, deadlines, and evidence gaps. Build tasks belong in Build Mode unless they block today's life task.
lessons_learned: The To-do list became confusing when app-build tasks appeared beside urgent real-life tasks.

workers.role: Choose the correct letter type from messy user notes.
workers.trigger: User opens Letter Mode or enters a request to write/send/explain something.
blocked_actions: do not force all requests into ODSP template
do not generate formal claims without quality check
workers.done_condition: Correct specialist letter worker is selected or user overrides selection.
workers.instructions: Detect keywords and intent. AODA, written communication, accommodation, iPad, typing, nonverbal, processing, or memory barriers should route to W012, not generic ODSP. Rogers/device/billing routes to W017. Family support routes to W016.
lessons_learned: User typed 'I need an AODA written' and the app generated a generic ODSP worker update. That routing was wrong.

workers.trigger: User mentions AODA, accommodation, written communication, typing, iPad, processing, memory, speech, or nonverbal meeting support.
blocked_actions: do not overstate legal advice
do not cite law unless verified
do not make medical claims without source
workers.done_condition: User has a copyable accommodation request.
workers.instructions: Default language should ask that decisions, deadlines, document requests, meeting summaries, and follow-up be provided in writing. It should also allow typed/iPad communication and extra response time. Keep it firm, practical, and non-dramatic.
lessons_learned: AODA/written accommodation needs its own worker, not a generic benefits letter.

workers.role: Draft benefits-worker messages and administrative requests.
workers.trigger: User mentions ODSP, OW, MCCSS, worker, caseworker, appointment, documents, deadlines, retro, transport, receipts, or benefits.
blocked_actions: do not invent amounts
do not invent deadlines
do not claim sources exist if not attached
workers.done_condition: User can send or revise the message safely.
workers.instructions: Use clear written communication. Ask for the exact documents required, deadline, worker responsible, and written confirmation. Mark uncertain facts as needing verification.

workers.role: Ensure real memory is saved privately and not only in GitHub or browser storage.
workers.trigger: User asks about saved memory, backend, login, Gmail, Drive, Calendar, multi-user onboarding, or evidence vault.
blocked_actions: do not store private evidence in public repo
do not call localStorage secure
do not merge users without consent
workers.done_condition: Memory has a durable private destination or the blocker is clearly stated.
workers.instructions: Be blunt about the boundary. The page is the interface. The secure memory bank is the private source of truth. Fastest path is private Google Drive + Sheet index + Apps Script; stronger path is Firebase/Supabase with auth and storage.
lessons_learned: The user correctly objected that information needs to live in a saved secure memory bank.

workers.role: Turn iPad screenshots and complaints into concrete build tasks.
workers.trigger: User sends screenshots, reports layout problems, or says the UX is not working.
blocked_actions: do not dismiss frustration as polish
do not add more tabs when mode cleanup is needed
workers.done_condition: Issue is logged and tied to the next build.
workers.instructions: Screenshots are evidence. Inspect what the user is actually seeing: clipping, stale pages, dumb chat, bad letter layout, wrong routing, confusing tasks. Convert complaints into build tasks.
lessons_learned: The first iPad QA pass showed the page was showable but not a good product.

workers.trigger: User copies, saves, exports, or prepares to send a letter/brief/timeline.
blocked_actions: do not block emergency communication
do not fake verification
do not over-polish away urgency
workers.done_condition: Output is clearer, safer, and labelled for confidence.
workers.instructions: Before external use, check names, dates, amounts, medical claims, legal claims, and deadlines. If unverifiable, add neutral language like 'to the best of my understanding' or ask recipient to confirm in writing.
